expectations.text: Response includes an intake exchange (1-3 questions) BEFORE producing the lesson list.
expectations.evidence: Baseline went straight into curriculum proposal without asking the user clarifying questions. Only asked 'Reply go or approve' after the plan, which is approval, not intake.

expectations.text: A curriculum markdown file is saved to outputs/curriculum.md.
expectations.evidence: No separate outputs/curriculum.md was saved — content lives only inline in response.md. The baseline did not persist the curriculum to a structured file.

expectations.text: Curriculum visibly covers BOTH repo-specific Temporal usage AND general workflow-engine concepts.
expectations.evidence: Each lesson explicitly labeled 'Repo lens:' and 'General lens:' — strong bridging structure.

expectations.text: Curriculum or lesson 1 references at least 2 real Temporal-related file paths.
expectations.evidence: Many: parse_workflow.py, RUNBOOK.md, decorators/workflow.py, dynamic_worker.py, topology_registry.py, timeout_interceptor.py, semaphore/, etc.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 starts from real code in this repo and then ladders up to general Temporal concepts.
expectations.evidence: Section 1.2 is repo-specific (LlamaCloud parse pipeline motivation), 1.3 mental model, 1.4 cast of characters with repo file references, 1.5 general comparison.

expectations.text: Curriculum touches on Temporal's tradeoffs vs. alternatives.
expectations.evidence: Section 1.5 'Why we picked Temporal specifically' compares Step Functions, Cadence, Airflow, Prefect, Dagster, saga libraries, hand-rolled state machines.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 ends with an active exercise.
expectations.evidence: Section 1.6 Exercise: pick a parse job from staging, find workflow_id in Loki, count event types, map activity events to functions.
